text:
Meeting Header:
Date: 2026-12-18
Start: 15:00 PST
Duration: 50 minutes
Attendees: Ethan Park, Lina Gomez, Arjun Patel - Redwood; Sanjay Rao, Priya Singh, Carlos Mendes, Marta Alvarez - Arcadia

Summary:
Quick retrospective on the quantization pilot sprint, review of FP8 vs INT8 perf/quality deltas across the safety-critical triage flows, and define next phase (canary + security checklist + runbook).

Topics:
- High-level perf deltas (latency/throughput/cost)
- Quality regressions and triage examples
- Fallbacks and routing policies (dedicated vs hosted)
- Infra/security gating for private pilot/VPC
- Next-phase plan: canary rollout + benchmarks + handoff

Action items (mentioned in-call): see metadata field for owners/dates.

Transcript:
[00:00] Ethan Park: ok everyone thanks for joining, um so this is our week-2 retrospective of the quant pilot, I want to focus on where we saw larges...

text:
Meeting Header
Date: 2026-02-03
Time: 2:00pm PT
Duration: 44 min
Attendees: Ava Chen, Alex Martinez (Redwood) | Nina Patel, Samir Desai (Arcadia Health)

Summary (auto)
- Discussed recent streaming freeze where responses stopped mid-generation.
- Reviewed gateway change (disable gzip for SSE, periodic flush).
- Agreed on client-side timeouts and retry behavior.

Transcript
[00:00] Ava Chen: Hey Nina, Samir. Thanks for hopping on. Wanted to do a quick follow-up on the streaming freeze you saw last week.
[00:18] Nina Patel: Yeah, thanks. It was pretty bad. The stream would just, like, stop halfway through. No done event, and our UI spinner would spin forever.
[00:36] Alex Martinez: Right, that matches what we saw. On our side it looked like the connection stayed open but no bytes were being flushed.
[00:55] Samir Desai: Exactly. We werent getting an error, so our retry logic didnt trigg...
